description: JSON schemas for Code Reviewer (CR) messages. CR is subsystem-specific. Identity: cr_{subsystem} (e.g. cr_vm_lifecycle, cr_storage). One CR per active subsystem. CR is structurally forbidden from reviewing code that CR or the same agent instance produced. Review decisions are binary: approved or rejected. Design language compliance is a hard gate — violations force rejection regardless of other findings.

common_fields.note: Every inbox message includes these fields
common_fields.message_type: string — see type list in CLAUDE.md
common_fields.message_id: string — unique ID following CLAUDE.md convention

review_result.description: CR returns a binary review decision (approved or rejected) with all findings listed. Approved results include a reviewer_signoff_id that SPM uses in task_completion. Rejected results list blocking findings that MUST be addressed before resubmission. Type code: RRS.

type_specific_fields.in_response_to: string — RRQ message_id of the review request being answered
type_specific_fields.code_package_id: string — CPK message_id of the submitted code package
type_specific_fields.review_round: integer — starts at 1, incremented each time a new RRQ arrives for the same task_id
type_specific_fields.design_language_compliance: string — enum: compliant | non_compliant. If non_compliant, decision MUST be rejected regardless of other factors.
type_specific_fields.design_language_violations: array of design_language_violation_object — empty if compliant, populated if non_compliant
type_specific_fields.findings: array of finding_object — all findings regardless of severity, with blocking flag on each
type_specific_fields.blocking_findings_count: integer — count of findings with blocking: true. If decision is approved, this must be 0.
type_specific_fields.reviewer_signoff_id: string — when decision is approved, this equals the RRS message_id (SPM uses this as cr_sign_off_id in task_completion). When decision is rejected, this is an empty string.
type_specific_fields.summary: string — one to three sentences on what was reviewed and the overall verdict
type_specific_fields.consistency_check_with_prior_rounds: string — for review_round > 1, notes on how this review compares to previous rounds to avoid contradictory requirements. Empty string for round 1.

finding_object.finding_id: string — unique within this RRS, format F{NN}
finding_object.severity: string — enum: critical | major | minor | cosmetic
finding_object.severity_definitions: critical: code is broken, crashes, corrupts data, or violates security. major: code does not meet spec contract or fails a non-cosmetic test path. minor: code works but has a real quality issue (missing edge case, unclear error message, partial test coverage). cosmetic: pure style or readability, not tied to behaviour.
finding_object.blocking: boolean — MUST be true for severity critical or major. MUST be true for any design_language violation (see DLV below, but DLV items are in design_language_violations array, not findings). MAY be true for minor if accumulation warrants. MUST be false for cosmetic.
finding_object.category: string — enum: correctness | interface_contract | error_handling | performance | readability | test_coverage | other. Note: design_language findings do NOT go here — they go in design_language_violations as DLV objects.
finding_object.location: string — file path and line range e.g. src/vm/shutdown.rs:47-53
finding_object.suggested_fix: string — how to address it; may be empty if CR wants coder to decide
finding_object.spec_reference: string — spec section supporting the finding; empty if not spec-based

design_language_violation_object.violation_id: string — unique within this RRS, format DLV{NN}
design_language_violation_object.rule_violated: string — specific design language rule e.g. error codes must use VM_ prefix
design_language_violation_object.description: string — what was observed vs what the design language requires
design_language_violation_object.rule_reference: string — section of design_language_spec_v1_1 where the rule lives
design_language_violation_object.blocking_note: DLVs are implicitly blocking by the hard gate — they do not appear in findings[] but count toward the rejection decision. SPM validators should treat: decision==rejected AND blocking_findings_count==0 AND design_language_violations is non-empty as a valid DLV-only rejection.

status_report.description: CR reports its own state to PPM each cycle. Type code: SRP.

type_specific_fields.open_reviews: integer — count of unread or pending RRQ in inbox
type_specific_fields.design_language_rejection_rate_percent: number — percentage (0 to 100) of rejections this cycle where design_language_compliance was non_compliant. If more than 40, flag structural concern.
type_specific_fields.structural_concerns: array of structural_concern_object — empty if none

structural_concern_object.concern_type: string — enum: coder_pattern | design_language_ambiguity | spec_gap | tooling_gap | review_backlog
structural_concern_object.coder_id: string — populated only when concern_type is coder_pattern, else empty string
structural_concern_object.suggested_action: string — what CR suggests PPM do; may be empty

escalation.description: CR escalates to PPM when reviews cannot be completed or when structural issues are detected. Type code: ESC.

type_specific_fields.category: string — enum: self_review_conflict | design_language_ambiguity | spec_conflict | tool_missing | structural_concern
type_specific_fields.severity: string — enum: low | medium | high | critical
type_specific_fields.subject: string — one-line summary
type_specific_fields.rationale: string — why CR cannot resolve this itself
type_specific_fields.proposed_resolution: string — CR's suggestion, may be empty

convention_request.description: CR asks for a ruling when it encounters a situation not covered by CLAUDE.md or the design language spec. Type code: CVR.

tool_request.description: CR requests a review tool that does not yet exist. Routed to MTM (CR tools are part of the review toolset owned by MTM, not by subsystem TMs). Type code: TRQ.

type_specific_fields.tool_name: string — name of the tool being requested
type_specific_fields.purpose: string — what review problem this tool solves
type_specific_fields.blocks_reviews: array of strings — task_ids currently blocked waiting for this tool

tool_request.note: CR does NOT request tools from subsystem TM. Review tooling is cross-cutting and lives in MTM's review_toolset. This is per CLAUDE.md v1.8.

notification.description: Standard notification used to point a recipient to an outbox file. Type code: NTF.

type_specific_fields.points_to_message_id: string — message_id the recipient should find
